summary: The correct answer excludes option D because while other methods significantly lower the risk of transmitting HIV via breastfeeding, fully ceasing this practice eliminates it entirely; thus making D distinct among preventive strategies for preventing vertical transmission effectively across various scenarios managed clinically today. Additionally, comprehensive care includes both medical intervention plus complete avoidance techniques such as formula feeding or pumping/bottle-feeding post-treatment phases when feasible ethically & medically speaking. This dual approach ensures maximum efficacy against potential risks associated specifically during lactation period without compromising overall well-being outcomes desired between affected mothers
